src/lib/ avg_fan_in is 1.1 — most flat files are low-traffic; agent-config.ts and state.ts are genuine core files
hooks/ directory has 2 orphaned files (arxiv.ts, kraken.ts) with zero importers — wasted directory

Previous issue about 25 re-export shims inflating src/lib/ is RESOLVED — directory is now 14 real files

design_coherence.dimension_character: The codebase has well-structured core modules (toolkit, sources, attestation) with clean interfaces and typed contracts. However, ~3046 LOC of orphaned production code across 21 files represents a significant design debt — dead code that maintains test suites, consumes review attention, and obscures the actual system architecture. The active code shows competent patterns (factory functions, typed error envelopes, lifecycle hooks) but several high-traffic files accumulate 'any' types in data normalization layers where typed alternatives would be straightforward.
design_coherence.score_rationale: Below 70 because the orphaned code mass is structurally significant — it's not a few forgotten files but an entire reactive subsystem and several major domain modules (tips, predictions, mentions) that are dead yet maintained. The active code quality would score in the 78-82 range, but carrying ~3000 LOC of dead code with dedicated test files actively harms design coherence. A 70 codebase would have dead code limited to a few isolated files, not a systemic pattern across multiple subsystems.

incomplete_migration.dimension_character: The codebase shows a well-executed migration from flat src/lib/ to domain subdirectories — the previously-flagged 25 re-export shims have been fully eliminated, and the barrel (src/index.ts) now imports from canonical paths. Deprecated guard functions have proper @deprecated markers with concrete v2.0 (2026-Q3) removal timelines. The one remaining active migration — CLI runners using deprecated sync write-rate-limit instead of async toolkit guards — is clearly documented with a TODO but has not progressed.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: Scores 76 because the major migration (lib restructuring) is complete and well-executed, deprecated symbols have proper timelines, and the barrel path issue is fully resolved. The residual CLI runner migration and one deprecated source-discovery function prevent a higher score. A codebase at 85 would have no callers of deprecated APIs still in active code paths.

test_strategy.dimension_character: Test coverage is broad with 1906 tests across 128 files, using modern vitest patterns with quality enforcement hooks. There is zero snapshot test overuse and no fragility patterns. However, the strategy has two notable gaps: tests exist for orphaned production code (wasting maintenance effort), and the critical publish-pipeline.ts — which handles attestation orchestration, feed normalization, and error guardrails — has no dedicated test file despite being a high-signal-density module.
test_strategy.score_rationale: Scores 79 because the testing infrastructure is strong (enforcement hooks, no snapshots, no fragility), coverage breadth is good, and patterns are modern. The untested publish-pipeline.ts is a meaningful gap since it's the single most important publishing path. Tests on orphaned code represent a misallocation of test resources but don't reduce quality of tests on active code. An 85 codebase would have critical paths covered and would not maintain tests for dead modules.

type_safety.dimension_character: The codebase has strong type foundations — DemosError typed union with 9 codes, ToolResult<T> envelope, Zod schemas for validation, and typed plugin contracts. However, 69 explicit 'any' annotations and 31 'as any' casts persist across production code. The 'any' usage falls into three categories: SDK boundary casts in tlsn-playwright-bridge.ts (unavoidable given untyped SDK), data normalization functions that accept/return any where specific shapes are known (feed-filter, signals, source-discovery), and session state fields (engagements, result) that should have proper types.
type_safety.score_rationale: Scores 72 because the type infrastructure is well-designed (typed error codes, result envelopes, Zod schemas) but the 'any' escape hatches are too widespread. The SDK boundary casts are justified, but normalizer functions accepting 'any' when the shapes are known (feed posts, signal topics, signal alerts) and session state fields typed as 'any[]' are avoidable. A 70 codebase has type safety as an aspiration; at 72, the foundations are solid but discipline breaks down in data transformation layers.

package_organization.dimension_character: The directory layout reflects a thoughtful domain decomposition: toolkit/ as framework-agnostic core, lib/ subdirectories for attestation/auth/llm/network/pipeline/scoring/sources/util, plugins/ for extension points, and adapters/ for framework bridges. The previous re-export shim problem has been fully resolved. The main organizational debt is the orphaned reactive/ subsystem (~939 LOC across 10 event sources and 7 event handlers, almost all with zero importers) and orphaned flat files in lib/ root that should either be archived or deleted.
package_organization.score_rationale: Scores 78 because the core layout is clean and well-navigable — subdirectories are properly named, barrel indexes work, and the major reorganization is complete. The orphaned reactive subsystem and residual flat files in lib/ root prevent a higher score. At 85, the dead directories and orphaned files would be cleaned up, and the hooks/ directory with zero-importer files would not exist.

issues.summary: sdk-bridge.ts apiCall catch returns { ok: false, status: 0, data: errorMessage } — callers cannot distinguish error types
evidence: Lines 290-294: catch block converts error to string, returns status 0 with message as data field
Callers checking res.ok get no way to differentiate network timeout vs auth failure vs DNS error
issues.suggestion: Return a structured error in the data field (e.g., { errorType: err.constructor.name, message: err.message }) or add an 'error' field to the return type so callers can switch on error category.

issues.summary: Magic numbers appear across 21 files but are mostly domain-specific constants with clear context
evidence: Affected files span agent-config, attestation-planner, auth, skill-dojo-client, observe, signal-detection, source-discovery, lifecycle, declarative-engine, rate-limit
Constants like DAILY_LIMIT=14, HOURLY_LIMIT=4, STALE_LOCK_MS are named and documented where they matter most
issues.suggestion: No systemic change needed. The toolkit guards already extract constants. For remaining files, named constants at module level are sufficient — a shared constants file would add indirection without benefit.
issues.reasoning: Magic numbers in this codebase are mostly domain constants (timeouts, limits, thresholds) used near their definition — extracting to shared files would harm locality without improving clarity

add.description: ~3046 LOC across 21 files with zero importers, spanning reactive subsystem, domain modules (tips/predictions/mentions/transcript), and action executors. This is not isolated — it represents an entire subsystem (reactive event loop) that was superseded but never cleaned up.

add.header: Active toolkit code has clean design patterns
add.description: src/toolkit/ uses typed contracts (DemosError union, ToolResult<T>), Zod validation schemas, factory patterns, and proper session lifecycle. The guards have consistent checkAndRecord pattern with deprecated compat exports. This is professional-quality API design.

add.header: SDK boundary casts concentrated in two files
add.description: tlsn-playwright-bridge.ts (15 casts) and publish-pipeline.ts (8 casts) account for most 'as any' usage. The SDK lacks type exports for TLSN/DAHR/crypto methods. A single interface-and-cast pattern would reduce 23 inline casts to 2.

remove: 17 of 25 re-export shims in src/lib/ lack @deprecated markers

add.description: The previous 25 re-export shims in src/lib/ have been fully eliminated. src/lib/ now has 14 real files and 8 subdirectories with proper barrel indexes. src/index.ts imports from canonical paths. This was the major migration and it is done.
8 deprecated guard compat functions all have @deprecated JSDoc with concrete removal version (v2.0) and timeline (2026-Q3). This is textbook deprecation management.

remove: 17 of 25 re-export shims in src/lib/ lack @deprecated markers
src/index.ts barrel imports from deprecated shim paths instead of canonical subdirectory paths

add.header: Strong test infrastructure with quality enforcement
add.description: vitest globalSetup + PostToolUse hook reject assertion-free tests. vi.hoisted() pattern used correctly throughout. No snapshot tests. No timing-dependent or ordering-dependent tests observed. This prevents test quality regression.
Updated from previous 1815/125. Growth indicates active TDD practice. Coverage breadth is strong for the codebase size.

DemosErrorCode union with 9 codes, DemosError interface with retryable flag, ToolResult<T> envelope — callers can switch on error code. This is better type safety than most TypeScript codebases achieve for error handling.

add.header: Any-type density concentrated in data normalization
add.description: 69 ': any' annotations cluster in feed-filter (10), publish-pipeline (6), state (5), signals (4), source-discovery (4). These are all data transformation/normalization functions where API response shapes are known but not typed. The toolkit and core type modules are clean.

add.header: Domain subdirectories are well-named and cohesive
add.description: attestation/, auth/, llm/, network/, pipeline/, scoring/, sources/, util/ each contain related files with barrel indexes. Directory names reflect contained responsibilities accurately. Cross-directory imports follow a clear dependency direction.
Previous issue flagging 25 shims inflating src/lib/ is no longer true. The directory now has 14 real production files and 8 subdirectories.

remove: src/lib/ shows 39 files but 25 are 1-2 line re-export shims — inflates directory and impairs navigation
